content: I'm still getting used to the time difference here in Berlin. It's been a few weeks since I moved, and everything is so different from what I was used to in the States.
Adjusting to a new city and time zone can be challenging. What brought you to Berlin, and how are you finding the city so far?
I moved for my job as an Environmental Policy Advisor. I'm working on a project to reduce carbon emissions in the city, which is really rewarding. My border collie, Thor, loves the parks here.
That sounds like important work. Berlin is known for its green spaces, so I'm sure Thor is enjoying the exploration. How do you like the German culture and food so far?

The food is great, although I do miss some American dishes. I've been trying to learn more German, but it's not easy. I've found a great bakery near my apartment that makes delicious sourdough, though.
Learning a new language can be tough, but immersion is a great way to pick it up. Sourdough is delicious. Have you had a chance to explore any of Berlin's aquariums or zoos with Thor?

Actually, yes. We visited the Berlin Aquarium last weekend, and it was amazing. Thor wasn't allowed inside, but he enjoyed the walk around the park. I've also been doing some drone photography in my free time.
Drone photography can capture some amazing perspectives. What kind of projects are you working on with your drone, and have you found any favorite spots in Berlin to fly?

I've been taking photos of the city's green roofs and parks to highlight the importance of urban green spaces. It's been a fun project. I'm planning to showcase some of the photos at a local environmental event on April 22nd.

That's a great way to use your hobby to support your professional goals. Earth Day is a significant event; I'm sure your photos will contribute positively to the discussion. How do you see your work in Berlin impacting the global environmental policy landscape?
I believe that every local action can have a ripple effect. By working on carbon reduction here, we can set an example for other cities. It's a challenging but rewarding field to be in.
It's inspiring to see individuals like you dedicated to making a difference. Do you have any plans to collaborate with other European cities on environmental initiatives?

Yes, we're in talks with a few cities to share best practices and possibly launch a joint initiative. It's exciting to think about the potential impact. I'm also looking forward to hiking more in the German countryside with Thor.

Collaboration is key in addressing global challenges. I wish you the best of luck with your projects and enjoy your hikes with Thor. Remember to take time to appreciate the beauty of nature around you.
I will, thanks. It's been great chatting with you. I feel a bit more settled now, knowing there are people interested in the same issues I care about.

It was my pleasure. Feel free to reach out anytime you need to discuss your projects or just want to talk about your experiences in Berlin. Good luck with everything, and I hope Thor enjoys all the upcoming adventures!
Thanks again, and yes, Thor is looking forward to them as well. I think we'll both love exploring more of what Berlin has to offer.
Berlin has a lot to offer, from its rich history to its vibrant cultural scene. I'm sure you and Thor will find plenty to enjoy. Stay in touch and let me know how your projects progress.
